comment_text.sn22.60:2.2: This view is attributed to the Bamboo-staffed Ascetic Gosāla, founder of the Ājīvakas, at DN 2:20.2. There, Pūraṇa Kassapa advocates the inefficacy of action (DN 2:16.1), while at SN 46.56 he is said to teach that there is no cause for knowledge.

root_text.sn22.60:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.sn22.60:1.3: Atha kho mahāli licchavi yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho mahāli licchavi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn22.60:2.4: Natthi hetu natthi paccayo sattānaṁ visuddhiyā;
root_text.sn22.60:3.1: “Atthi, mahāli, hetu atthi paccayo sattānaṁ saṅkilesāya;
root_text.sn22.60:3.3: Atthi, mahāli, hetu, atthi paccayo sattānaṁ visuddhiyā;
root_text.sn22.60:4.1: “Katamo pana, bhante, hetu katamo paccayo sattānaṁ saṅkilesāya;
root_text.sn22.60:5.1: “Rūpañca hidaṁ, mahāli, ekantadukkhaṁ abhavissa dukkhānupatitaṁ dukkhāvakkantaṁ anavakkantaṁ sukhena, nayidaṁ sattā rūpasmiṁ sārajjeyyuṁ.
root_text.sn22.60:5.2: Yasmā ca kho, mahāli, rūpaṁ sukhaṁ sukhānupatitaṁ sukhāvakkantaṁ anavakkantaṁ dukkhena, tasmā sattā rūpasmiṁ sārajjanti;
root_text.sn22.60:5.4: Ayaṁ kho, mahāli, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo sattānaṁ saṅkilesāya;
root_text.sn22.60:6.1: Vedanā ca hidaṁ, mahāli, ekantadukkhā abhavissa dukkhānupatitā dukkhāvakkantā anavakkantā sukhena, nayidaṁ sattā vedanāya sārajjeyyuṁ.
root_text.sn22.60:6.2: Yasmā ca kho, mahāli, vedanā sukhā sukhānupatitā sukhāvakkantā anavakkantā dukkhena, tasmā sattā vedanāya sārajjanti;
root_text.sn22.60:6.5: Ayampi kho, mahāli, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo sattānaṁ saṅkilesāya.
root_text.sn22.60:7.2: saṅkhārā ca hidaṁ, mahāli, ekantadukkhā abhavissaṁsu dukkhānupatitā dukkhāvakkantā anavakkantā sukhena, nayidaṁ sattā saṅkhāresu sārajjeyyuṁ.
root_text.sn22.60:7.3: Yasmā ca kho, mahāli, saṅkhārā sukhā sukhānupatitā sukhāvakkantā anavakkantā dukkhena, tasmā sattā saṅkhāresu sārajjanti;
root_text.sn22.60:7.6: Ayampi kho, mahāli, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo sattānaṁ saṅkilesāya.
root_text.sn22.60:8.1: Viññāṇañca hidaṁ, mahāli, ekantadukkhaṁ abhavissa dukkhānupatitaṁ dukkhāvakkantaṁ anavakkantaṁ sukhena, nayidaṁ sattā viññāṇasmiṁ sārajjeyyuṁ.
root_text.sn22.60:8.2: Yasmā ca kho, mahāli, viññāṇaṁ sukhaṁ sukhānupatitaṁ sukhāvakkantaṁ anavakkantaṁ dukkhena, tasmā sattā viññāṇasmiṁ sārajjanti;
root_text.sn22.60:8.4: Ayampi kho, mahāli, hetu ayaṁ paccayo sattānaṁ saṅkilesāya.
root_text.sn22.60:9.1: “Katamo pana, bhante, hetu katamo paccayo sattānaṁ visuddhiyā;
root_text.sn22.60:9.3: “Rūpañca hidaṁ, mahāli, ekantasukhaṁ abhavissa sukhānupatitaṁ sukhāvakkantaṁ anavakkantaṁ dukkhena, nayidaṁ sattā rūpasmiṁ nibbindeyyuṁ.
root_text.sn22.60:9.4: Yasmā ca kho, mahāli, rūpaṁ dukkhaṁ dukkhānupatitaṁ dukkhāvakkantaṁ anavakkantaṁ sukhena, tasmā sattā rūpasmiṁ nibbindanti;
root_text.sn22.60:9.6: Ayaṁ kho, mahāli, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo, sattānaṁ visuddhiyā.
root_text.sn22.60:10.1: Vedanā ca hidaṁ, mahāli, ekantasukhā abhavissa …pe…
root_text.sn22.60:10.3: saṅkhārā ca hidaṁ, mahāli, ekantasukhā abhavissaṁsu …pe…
root_text.sn22.60:10.4: viññāṇañca hidaṁ, mahāli, ekantasukhaṁ abhavissa sukhānupatitaṁ sukhāvakkantaṁ anavakkantaṁ dukkhena, nayidaṁ sattā viññāṇasmiṁ nibbindeyyuṁ.
root_text.sn22.60:10.5: Yasmā ca kho, mahāli, viññāṇaṁ dukkhaṁ dukkhānupatitaṁ dukkhāvakkantaṁ anavakkantaṁ sukhena, tasmā sattā viññāṇasmiṁ nibbindanti;
root_text.sn22.60:10.7: Ayaṁ kho, mahāli, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo, sattānaṁ visuddhiyā.

translation_text.sn22.60:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.sn22.60:1.3: Then Mahāli the Licchavi went up to the Buddha … and said to him:
translation_text.sn22.60:2.2: ‘There is no cause or reason for the corruption of sentient beings.
translation_text.sn22.60:2.3: Sentient beings are corrupted without cause or reason.
translation_text.sn22.60:2.4: There’s no cause or reason for the purification of sentient beings.
translation_text.sn22.60:2.5: Sentient beings are purified without cause or reason.’
translation_text.sn22.60:2.6: What does the Buddha say about this?”
translation_text.sn22.60:3.1: “Mahāli, there is a cause and reason for the corruption of sentient beings.
translation_text.sn22.60:3.2: Sentient beings are corrupted with cause and reason.
translation_text.sn22.60:3.3: There is a cause and reason for the purification of sentient beings.
translation_text.sn22.60:3.4: Sentient beings are purified with cause and reason.”
translation_text.sn22.60:4.1: “But sir, what is the cause and reason for the corruption of sentient beings?
translation_text.sn22.60:4.2: How are sentient beings corrupted with cause and reason?”
translation_text.sn22.60:5.1: “Mahāli, if form were exclusively painful—soaked and steeped in pain and not steeped in pleasure—sentient beings wouldn’t be aroused by it.
translation_text.sn22.60:5.2: But because form is pleasurable—soaked and steeped in pleasure and not steeped in pain—sentient beings are aroused by it.
translation_text.sn22.60:5.3: Since they are aroused by it, they’re caught up in it, and so they become corrupted.
translation_text.sn22.60:5.4: This is a cause and reason for the corruption of sentient beings.
translation_text.sn22.60:5.5: This is how sentient beings are corrupted with cause and reason.
translation_text.sn22.60:8.1: consciousness were exclusively painful—soaked and steeped in pain and not steeped in pleasure—sentient beings wouldn’t be aroused by it.
translation_text.sn22.60:8.2: But because consciousness is pleasurable—soaked and steeped in pleasure and not steeped in pain—sentient beings are aroused by it.
translation_text.sn22.60:8.3: Since they are aroused by it, they’re caught up in it, and so they become corrupted.
translation_text.sn22.60:8.4: This is a cause and reason for the corruption of sentient beings.
translation_text.sn22.60:8.5: This is how sentient beings are corrupted with cause and reason.”
translation_text.sn22.60:9.1: “But sir, what is the cause and reason for the purification of sentient beings?
translation_text.sn22.60:9.2: How are sentient beings purified with cause and reason?”
translation_text.sn22.60:9.3: “Mahāli, if form was perfectly pleasurable—soaked and steeped in pleasure and not steeped in pain—sentient beings wouldn’t grow disillusioned with it.
translation_text.sn22.60:9.4: But because form is painful—soaked and steeped in pain and not steeped in pleasure—sentient beings do grow disillusioned with it.
translation_text.sn22.60:9.5: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they are purified.
translation_text.sn22.60:9.6: This is a cause and reason for the purification of sentient beings.
translation_text.sn22.60:9.7: This is how sentient beings are purified with cause and reason.
translation_text.sn22.60:10.4: consciousness was perfectly pleasurable—soaked and steeped in pleasure and not steeped in pain—sentient beings wouldn’t grow disillusioned with it.
translation_text.sn22.60:10.5: But because consciousness is painful—soaked and steeped in pain and not steeped in pleasure—sentient beings do grow disillusioned with it.
translation_text.sn22.60:10.6: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they are purified.
translation_text.sn22.60:10.7: This is a cause and reason for the purification of sentient beings.
translation_text.sn22.60:10.8: This is how sentient beings are purified with cause and reason.”
